Welcome everyone to the longer version of the Diagnostic Sale. It is a two-page sale. Try to keep this as short and as easy as humanly possible. I think that you should print this out on double-sided paper and put them on clipboards so that the person who is actually doing the sale can literally read the questions. So the intention of this sale is to make this as scalable as possible and as easy as possible to delegate. I know that Dylan already handed this over to a salesperson and they closed like a $2400 sale in their first try who I've never sold before. So if you don't overthink this, it is a very, very, very simple process. And so you're literally just going to ask the questions. The reason I set it up this way was with the little black dots is because I think with your eyes it's easier to kind of check off the questions that you've already asked. And then you can write answers in the little gap that's underneath. And that way it looks like it's much more of an intake kind of questionnaire back and forth rather than you interrogating them or selling them. So I have the pre-meeting checklist just to make sure that any of your people who are working with you have already done, they filled out the pre-framed questionnaire, they noted their bad habit cost section, making sure that we've covered any of the costs that their bad habits might be costing so we can use those at the end to get them, you know, overcome price issues, show them before and afters, and then get scale and body fat stuff. So next is, hey, the quick one is like, all right, cool. Before you start it, is this decision you can make on your own is for someone else's approval that you need? And usually they're like, oh, I can do it. They're like, okay, great. So something happened, their kid said something, those are great, let me get those ones. But he'd be like, you know what, it's been a while and I've been meaning to do this and I need to do it, right? And so then that, they basically tell you the urgency so they can get around the, I need to think about it later. So this is all we're doing is we're overcoming the overcomes that would happen later, we're just doing it the front of the set. All right, so we'll brought you today, it seems like the problem with your goal, they tell you your goal, great. So your goal is you're trying to lose weight, tone up, gain muscle, you're currently X, you're trying to get to Y, great, we do this all the time. Why now, what changed, why today, okay, understand. So why is this important to you, right? Well like, what is not being in shape preventing you from doing right now that you want to do? All right, which is the second question. And then they will tell you what they are not able to do that they wish they could do. This is their deeper emotional feeling of what they're trying to do. And you're like, got it, okay, I understand. And whenever you're doing each one of these, you just want to repeat back to them what they just said, they will feel like you're listening to them in a much higher degree, you'll build up for way faster. All right, identify the gap. So why have you been successful in the past, right? What's been missing from the things that you've tried? All right, all of what we're trying to get them to say here is just they're missing fitness, they're missing nutrition, they're missing any kind of body, which basically just means they didn't know what they were doing, or they didn't stick with the thing they were doing, right? Right. Okay, cool. And what you can do is you can tell them the kind of story that goes along with each of them. Like we only do the things that we enjoy doing. So do you, I mean, do you think that you're going to be able to stick with chicken and broccoli the rest of your life? No, of course not. Like, okay, cool. So I mean, what we found is that people have to like the foods that they're eating. I know crazy concept for them in order to stick with it. Right. Like basically no humans eat chicken and broccoli for the rest of their life. It doesn't happen. Right. It doesn't happen. It'll definitely get you to lose weight fast, but you're not going to keep it off. Right. So what we have to do is figure out how you can hide your cake and eat it too. What I mean is have the body you want and eat cake. Right. So the reason that people have the, you know, like there's three stories here you can tell any of them. You can tell an insurance story, which is like having an accountability coach is just to make sure it's the insurance. That way you know that you're going to stick with it and just fall off the insurance policy coverage. You can get to your right back with a rope. Yeah. And that's why the best athletes in the world still have coaches. Right. And so it's the biggest lie in the world that people think that they're not going to have a coach. Right. Or they think that they don't need a coach when the reality is that every single person who achieves has a coach. Right. And that's why you weren't able to stick with the past. Right. Because no one checked in on it. Of course you weren't. Right. What happens when you plateau? You don't know. Right. That's why it's good to have somebody who's already done this a hundred times. So the question is like, do you want to solve this for good and be done with it and not do all these cheap things that you've done in the past? Because you know that all you're going to do is come back here in six months or a year or three years, five, 10, 15, 20 pounds heavier. Right? Right. Okay, cool. And so that's basically negative future pacing. So it's like, cool. So let's walk out the door today as though you don't sign up and see what your life looks like in a year with you gaining 20 more pounds. So you're 180 now. What if you're 200 a year from now? How much harder is it going to be then? Right? So the thing is, we know that you need these things. Period. You need them. So like now let's look at the bad habit stuff. Let's look at how much money you're going to save. Like let's work on this. As long as it's not the first thing of like, you don't believe in me. You don't believe in the program. You don't think you can do it, whatever it is. Like once you attack those things, then it's like, cool. Then let's make this work. Then you're working with them to close the sale. If it's a spouse thing, do they know that you want to get in shape and fail? Yes. Are they happy that you're not succeeding? No, of course not. So do you think it would be unreasonable to think that they would support you in doing something that would make you happy? No. So you're kind of using past logic to say that they're probably going to be in support of you doing something if they know they don't like something else. Like they don't approve of you being super unhappy, right? Of course not. So then why would they disapprove of you doing something about it? So that's kind of the whole point with the spouse close. And then you can also ask, what's their main concern? Because then it gets it away from them and then usually they'll use their husband as a foil as a dummy that they can say they have a concern. It's like, well, what's their main concern? It's like, well, I don't think they'll think that I'm going to stick with it. Okay, cool. And then you address that point rather than addressing the husband, right? And then there, if you really read it, I'd be like, remember, we guarantee you this, like you hit like, I'm guaranteeing you hit it. Like when you pay the money, you're going to get there and I'm going to keep service you until you hit it provided you do X, Y, Z. Right? Last case you do delay pay. Price wise, it's really just, do you really want to lose this weight for them? Right? It's reestablishing the value and then mentioning the savings that go along with the site. Right now your current lifestyle is costing you more than this well. So you only save money while you're doing this. So I wouldn't make money is never a concern. Right? Like money is never an issue because we're going to save you so much money on all the other things that you're not going to do. Right? And then you can go into vote with our dollars. You know, we, we vote our dollars with things we care about. So literally everything besides your rent, we can, we can cover. You know what I mean? Like literally everything, everything we can figure out if this is a priority for you. Right? Because if it's not, then that's a totally different thing. But I know when you came in today, you said X, Y, and Z and I just want to make sure that is that still, is that still how you feel? And we're like, yeah, you're right. And like, okay, now that's where rapport is really important for these. Now, just as an overarching question here, whenever you get into obstacles, whenever people start, you get into the, into the, into the fist fight, you have to remember that it's not combative. You cannot change your tone. If anything, you have to relax more. And it's kind of like in a fight for like people short in their breath, they, they, they, they breathe too fast. They, they get too tense, right? When they're adrenaline spikes. So this is where practice comes into it. But the more you get into these sales, the more you, you have to get more childlike. More curious when you ask these questions. So, no, no way. What's your main concern? Right? Totally caring. Not like, like, well, I mean, you're going to have to do this anyways. Like, why are you, you know, like it's not a Q and a S or you'll never close someone. No, you have to be genuinely on their side, asking questions so that they come to the conclusion that we all know is that they're just afraid to make the decision. And all we're here to do is help them make it so that we can help them. All right. So that is how this works. We include the service guarantee, which means that when they hit the goal that we will service them for that X period of time provided they do X, Y and Z. If they don't do X, Y and Z, then the guarantee is void. That's not, that's not on you anymore. Right? But if someone does all of those things and takes the supplements through that period of time, the reason you do the service guarantee is because if you are serving for servicing someone past that guarantee and they're still in the supplements, you're still getting paid to service them. Okay. And so that's a key point. And most people after 30 weeks, if they've done a great job and literally had done all that stuff, it might take you an extra month to hit there. But that's going to be a very, very, very, very, very small minority of cases. And be realistic with the goals that you're setting. You know, like be realistic. You're going to guarantee it. Be realistic. You know, that is the, that is the diagnostic sale, the longer version. Cole, my sales manager who turned around a lot of 24s, made a second one, he and I work together on a second one that's shorter. It's one page. For once people kind of get this one down and really understand some of these questions because now all like this is where the skill of the salesman comes in. It's like, it's never the best this year in a tall life. Right. Like, and so like, you kind of got to judge who you're talking to. But otherwise, this is a script to ask the questions. It'll lead them naturally to the conclusion. And with this kind of pricing, you'll be able to close way more, way higher ticket, which is the entire objective of this selling script. So use wisely.
